mechanism_narrative: Higher unemployment rate directly signals weaker aggregate demand, reduced corporate earnings, and risk-off sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The relationship is monotonic across all levels.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative in periods when UNRATE is above its median level, or rank IC over 3-year rolling windows falls below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A step increase in payroll employment by more than 200,000 in a single month signals accelerating labor demand and economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is not positive and at least 1.0% in instances where PAYEMS MoM > 200k, or hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high CPI inflation and very low fed funds rates imply negative real interest rates that overstimulate demand, create asset bubbles, and lead to a correction in equities as financial conditions tighten, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative forward returns in triggered instances do not exceed the unconditional negative return rate by more than 5 percentage points, or average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when unemployment is already low, further strong payroll gains signal overheating, wage inflation, and margin compression, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS YoY growth > 2%)
failure_condition: In the defined late-cycle regime, positive payroll months do not lead to below-median or negative 20-day returns, or the regime-conditional Sharpe ratio is not lower than in early-cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in retail sales by more than 0.5% reflects strong consumer demand and economic momentum, triggering short-term buying pressure that lifts the S&P 500 over the upcoming 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after trigger is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), or win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts by more than 5% signals deteriorating demand, construction sector weakness, and an eventual economic downturn, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the cycle turns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after trigger are not negative on average, or the signal's information coefficient (IC) over rolling 3-year windows is above 0.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation rising above 3% triggers market fears of monetary tightening, rising discount rates, and compressed equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the repricing propagates.
failure_condition: Average 60-day return after trigger is not negative, or rolling 5-year IC is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and housing starts increase month-over-month simultaneously, it confirms broad-based economic expansion with synchronized supply and demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns after joint positive triggers are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean, or the long-short spread between joint-positive and joint-negative months is less than 2%.
